mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the unemployment rate below 4% signals a tightening labor market, increasing corporate profit margins and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE falls below 4% despite the signal.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in industrial production (INDPRO) and rising oil prices (CPIAUCSL) signal supply-side shocks that increase inflation uncertainty, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where INDPRO declines and CPIAUCSL rises simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in housing starts (HOUST) above 1.6 million units signals strong demand for construction, boosting equity valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances where HOUST exceeds 1600.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) above 150k per month signals robust employment growth, boosting consumer spending and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 50%+ of months with PAYEMS growth above 150k.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production (INDPRO) below 100 signals weakening manufacturing activity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances where INDPRO declines below 100.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% and federal funds rates (FEDFUNDS) above 4% signal a tightening monetary environment, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both exceed thresholds.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real sales (RSAFS) below 620 billion dollars signals weakening consumer demand, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 50%+ of instances where RSAFS declines below 620.
